title: Module 1
content:
Intel 8051: Architecture - ALU, internal RAM and ROM, Organization
of ports. Registers of 8051: data pointer, program counter, CPU
registers, register banks, stack and stack pointer, PSW, Special Function
Registers.   Addressing modes of 8051. Instruction set of 8051-
Assembler directives, Data transfer instructions, Arithmetic, logical,
compare and rotate instructions- Bit processing instructions- Program
flow control instructions

title: Module 2
content:
Programming using Assembly language:  Illustrative examples using
of all types of instructions. Programming the ports of 8051.
Programming in C: Illustrative examples of C programming for 8051-
time delay and I/O operations, logic operations

title: Module 3
content:
Timers of 8051: All modes of operations, programming the timers in
assembly language and C language, timer as an event counter. Serial
Communication with 8051: Introduction to serial communication,
RS232 protocol. Serial communication programming in assembly
language. Interrupt system of 8051: introduction to interrupts-
interrupts in 8051-priority of interrupts- Interrupt handling and
execution for timer, external and serial interrupts. Programming to
handle all types of interrupts in assembly language

title: Module 4
content:
Interfacing of 8051: LCD, stepper motor, ADC, 8255. Introduction
to AVR microcontroller: Overview of AVR family, ATmega32 –
Architectural overview, CPU core, memory organization. Introduction
to
PIC
microcontrollers:
Architectural
features,
Memory
organization, Core and peripheral features
Project based learning: 9 Hrs
•
In the first class, students shall be given an awareness and/ or guidelines on how to
implement learn this course through a project and form groups of maximum 5 members in
the first week.
•
Each group identify a project where microcontrollers can be used for simple biomedical
applications and perform a literature survey.
•
Choose a suitable microcontroller/ microcontroller- based development boards for your
application and the input-output devices to be interfaced
•
Develop the block diagram and present before the jury within first month after the
commencement of classes
•      Implement necessary hardware and software to develop the prototype
•      Prepare a report and present before the jury for final evaluation.
